policy_name: AICTE Saksham Scholarship Scheme For Specially-Abled Student (Degree)

sections.overview: Saksham is a scholarship scheme by the Ministry of Education implemented by AICTE to support specially-abled students in pursuing technical education. It provides financial assistance to help students continue higher education and build a successful career.
sections.objective: The scheme aims to encourage specially-abled students to pursue technical education and ensure equal opportunities by reducing financial barriers.
eligibility: Admission in first year or second year (lateral entry) of degree course in AICTE-approved institutions
Students with disability of not less than 40%
Family income should not exceed Rs. 8,00,000 per annum
Valid income certificate issued by state or UT government
benefits: Duration up to 4 years for first year students and 3 years for lateral entry students
Covers tuition fees, computer purchase, books, stationery, equipment, and software
Disbursed annually through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT)
Renewal based on academic progress and submission through National Scholarship Portal
application_process: Visit National Scholarship Portal https://scholarships.gov.in/otrapplication/#/ and register
Complete mobile verification and e-KYC process
Generate OTR using NSP OTR app and Aadhaar FaceRD app
Login using OTR and fill application form
Upload required documents and submit application
Track payment status on PFMS portal https://pfms.nic.in/static/NewLayoutCommonContent.aspx?RequestPagename=static/TrackNSPpayments.aspx
documents_required: Diploma or ITI certificate for lateral entry
exclusions: Students not promoted to next academic year
Gap more than two years between qualifying exam and admission
Students receiving any other scholarship or financial assistance
Students who drop out or fail
Students enrolled in non-AICTE approved courses or institutions

faq.question: Can a 3rd year student apply for Saksham Scholarship?
faq.answer: No, only first year or second year (lateral entry) students are eligible.

faq.question: Can students already receiving another scholarship apply?
faq.answer: No, students receiving any government scholarship are not eligible.

faq.question: How is the scholarship amount disbursed?
faq.answer: It is disbursed through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the student's bank account.

faq.question: Is Aadhaar mandatory?
faq.answer: Yes, Aadhaar and Aadhaar-linked bank account are mandatory.

faq.question: Is there a helpline for queries?
Whom to contact for portal issues?
What are common reasons for application rejection?
faq.answer: Reasons include incomplete application, non-AICTE approved course/institute, dual degree enrollment, receiving other scholarships, or missing valid disability certificate.
